coach_message: This move leads to a different opening than the one you selected.
why_wrong: It’s not necessarily bad—just a different study path with different plans.
hint: If you want to train this opening, follow the starting move order of the selected module.

coach_message: This develops a piece, but it misses the key idea of this position.
why_wrong: In this spot, priority is usually center control / opening lines / solving an immediate threat.
hint: Ask: “What’s the main positional goal here (center, king safety, tactic)?”

coach_message: This is playable, but it’s slower than the main plan you’re training.
why_wrong: You may concede space/initiative or allow the opponent an easy plan.
hint: Look for the active move that improves development or challenges the center.

coach_message: This creates unnecessary king weaknesses at an early stage.
why_wrong: Flank pawn pushes can weaken critical squares and delay development/castling.
hint: Prefer development and king safety first; attack later when pieces are out.

coach_message: Too early for the queen—your opponent can gain tempo by attacking it.
why_wrong: Early queen moves often allow easy development with threats on the queen.
hint: Develop minor pieces first; bring the queen out when it has a clear purpose.

coach_message: This move switches into a different branch than the line you selected.
why_wrong: It may be good, but it won’t match the line you’re trying to memorize/practice.
hint: If you want this branch, select it as a separate line.
